message:
Last weekend I realized our go-bags were still packed with snacks from 2019. So instead of just quietly freaking out, I made it a whole thing—threw on a playlist, grabbed the kids, and we did a 'preparedness picnic' on the living room floor. Sorted through old granola bars (RIP), updated documents, checked flashlight batteries, and honestly? It was kind of nice spending that time together, and everyone felt a little more settled knowing we're actually ready.

Turns out prep doesn't have to be stressful. Sometimes it's just an excuse to gather, check in, and make sure you've got each other covered.

How do you all tackle the "boring prep stuff" without it feeling like a chore?
